requirements.heading: Edge cases

coveredRequirements: Currently worktree cleanup needs the exact worktree slug to cleanup the worktree. I want to add the option that one can simply send /worktree-cleanup and then as a followup can select which worktrees should be cleaned up.
Add Extensions in a way that fits the existing repository structure and conventions.
Unconfirmed product changes beyond the requested feature
Acceptance criteria cover the primary user-visible flow and at least one edge case.
The plan cites affected modules or explicitly notes when no prior module exists.
ambiguousRequirements: The feature behavior is documented in repo-grounded terms. — This is a documentation quality requirement, not a runtime behavior. Covered indirectly by verifying the cited module exists and exposes the right command.

No obvious existing module is found for the request. — This edge case describes the planning process itself (what if there's no module to modify). Not testable as runtime behavior since extensions/worktree.ts does exist.
Critical behavior remains ambiguous and needs clarification before implementation. — This edge case describes the planning process (when to ask questions). Not a runtime feature behavior.
(none) under Clarified decisions — There are no clarified decisions to test; this is explicitly empty in the plan.
notes: Fix 1 (race condition): Removed all setTimeout fallbacks in ui.custom mocks. The done callback is now called synchronously exactly once after renderer() is invoked, eliminating the race condition between renderer's internal done() call and the timeout.

Fix 2 (import smoke tests): Removed all three dynamic import tests for sibling test files. Replaced them with fs.existsSync checks that verify file presence without executing test registrations or causing side effects.
Fix 3 (assumed ctx shape): Added a prominent NOTE comment at the top of the file acknowledging the assumed context shape and noting the /ss command pattern reference from the plan.

Fix 4 (empty selection edge case): Added a dedicated test 'worktree-cleanup interactive mode with empty selection does not remove any worktrees and notifies user' that simulates done([]) and asserts no removal commands are issued.
Fix 5 (Clarified decisions = none): This is explicitly empty in the plan. Listed in ambiguousRequirements. No runtime test possible.

Fix 6 (plan cites affected modules): Added test 'the affected module extensions/worktree.ts exists and provides the worktree-cleanup command as cited in the plan' verifying the cited module exists on disk and exposes the expected command.

Fix 7 (no obvious existing module): This is a planning-process edge case about what happens when there's no module to modify. Since extensions/worktree.ts does exist, this is not a runtime-testable behavior. Listed in ambiguousRequirements.
Fix 8 (critical behavior remains ambiguous): This is a planning-process edge case about when to seek clarification. Not a runtime feature. Listed in ambiguousRequirements.
